# GitHub Copilot — Usage Examples
# Full reference for commands, modifiers, context references, and combinations

---

## 📎 Context Reference Quick Reference

| You Want To Reference         | Use This                              |
|------------------------------|---------------------------------------|
| Currently open file          | `#editor`                             |
| Selected/highlighted code    | `#selection`                          |
| A file by name               | `#OrderService.java`                  |
| A file by path               | `#file:'src/main/java/OrderService.java'` |
| Specific lines in a file     | `#OrderService.java:40-80`            |
| A specific method            | `#processCheckout`                    |
| A specific class             | `#PaymentService`                     |
| Entire workspace             | `@workspace`                          |
| Entire GitHub repo           | `@github` (Enterprise only)           |

---

## ⚡ Depth Mode Prefixes

```
? What is the difference between @Component and @Bean?
```
> Quick mode — 3 to 5 bullets, no headers, no ceremony.

```
What is the difference between @Component and @Bean?
```
> Standard mode — structured explanation with code examples.

```
!! Should we use event-driven or direct service calls for our order pipeline?
```
> Deep Dive — full analysis, failure modes, alternatives, trade-offs, migration path.

---

## 🌐 Scale Overrides

```
Scale: batch
Command: copilot-review
#editor
```

```
Scale: low-traffic
Command: copilot-perf
#OrderReportService.java
```

```
Scale: 20k req/sec
Command: copilot-arch

Design a rate-limiting service across 50 pods.
```

---

## 📋 OutputFormat Modifier

```
Command: copilot-review
OutputFormat: checklist
#editor
```
> Returns all findings as `- [ ] ClassName#method — Severity — Problem` checkboxes.

```
Command: copilot-review
FocusAreas: performance, security
OutputFormat: table
#OrderService.java
```
> Returns findings as a markdown table: Location | Severity | Problem | Fix.

```
Command: copilot-review
OutputFormat: summary-only
#editor
```
> Returns a single short paragraph — useful for quick triage before a full review.

---

## 🔎 Command: copilot-review

### Currently open file
```
Command: copilot-review
#editor
```

### Specific file by name
```
Command: copilot-review
#OrderService.java
```

### Specific file by path
```
Command: copilot-review
#file:'src/main/java/com/example/service/OrderService.java'
```

### Specific method only
```
Command: copilot-review
Scope: OrderService#processCheckout
#OrderService.java
```

### Specific line range
```
Command: copilot-review
FocusAreas: concurrency, security
#OrderService.java:120-185
```

### Multiple files at once
```
Command: copilot-review
FocusAreas: data-flow correctness

Review the full call chain across these files:
#OrderController.java
#OrderService.java
#OrderMapper.java
```

### Entire workspace — cross-cutting concern
```
Command: copilot-review
FocusAreas: security
@workspace

Find all places where user input is used in SQL queries or file paths.
```

### Baseline data flow before refactoring
```
Command: copilot-review
FocusAreas: performance, database, data flow correctness
Phase: baseline
#OrderService.java

Analyze baseline data flow before any refactoring.
Identify:
- Database write paths
- Execution ordering
- Transaction boundaries
- Side effect risks
```

### Currently selected code only
```
Command: copilot-review
FocusAreas: correctness
#selection
```

### Mid-refactor — do not flag intentional temporary state
```
Command: copilot-review
FocusAreas: correctness, concurrency
Context: refactor-in-progress
#OrderDispatchService.java

Reviewing only the new async dispatch path.
Ignore the legacy synchronous fallback — it will be removed.
```

### Legacy code you cannot modify
```
Command: copilot-review
FocusAreas: security
Context: legacy-readonly
#LegacyPaymentGateway.java

Flag risks only. Suggest wrapper mitigations — do not suggest internal changes.
```

### Prototype — skip noise
```
Command: copilot-review
SkipSections: testability, observability
Context: prototype
#editor
```

### Deep dive on a critical service
```
!! Command: copilot-review
FocusAreas: database, concurrency
Phase: full
Scale: 10k req/sec
#InventoryReservationService.java
```

---

## 🐛 Command: copilot-debug

### Currently open file with stack trace
```
Command: copilot-debug
#editor

Exception:
  java.lang.IllegalStateException: Transaction already active

Occurs under load on the /checkout endpoint.
Stack trace: [paste here]
```

### Specific method + stack trace
```
Command: copilot-debug
FocusAreas: root-cause, fix
#OrderService.java:processCheckout

NullPointerException thrown intermittently.
Stack trace: [paste here]
```

### CompletableFuture hang — reference executor config
```
Command: copilot-debug
FocusAreas: root-cause, prevention
#OrderDispatchService.java
#AsyncConfig.java

Pipeline hangs after ~2 minutes under high load.
No exception thrown. Service becomes unresponsive.
```

### MySQL deadlock — reference service and mapper
```
Command: copilot-debug
FocusAreas: root-cause, fix
#InventoryService.java
#InventoryMapper.java

Deadlock detected between `inventory` and `orders` tables.
SHOW ENGINE INNODB STATUS: [paste here]
```

### Full deep dive with baseline
```
!! Command: copilot-debug
Phase: full
#CheckoutOrchestrator.java

Map the full execution path before diagnosing.
Identify all state mutations, async handoffs, and lock acquisitions.
Stack trace: [paste here]
```

### Selected stack trace + context
```
Command: copilot-debug
FocusAreas: fix, test
#selection

This is the failing method. Diagnose and provide a regression test.
```

---

## ♻️ Command: copilot-refactor

### Currently open file
```
Command: copilot-refactor
#editor
```

### Specific class
```
Command: copilot-refactor
#ReportingService.java
```

### Baseline before splitting a large class
```
Command: copilot-refactor
Phase: baseline
#OrderProcessingService.java

Map all responsibilities, coupling points, and risk zones
before suggesting how to split this class.
```

### Focus on concurrency only
```
Command: copilot-refactor
FocusAreas: concurrency
#InventoryManager.java

Only improve thread safety. Do not change business logic or structure.
```

### Multiple related files
```
Command: copilot-refactor
FocusAreas: structure, solid

Refactor to properly separate concerns across:
#OrderController.java
#OrderService.java
#OrderValidator.java
```

### Mid-refactor in progress
```
Command: copilot-refactor
Context: refactor-in-progress
FocusAreas: structure, solid
#OrderService.java

The DB layer has already been extracted.
Now focus on splitting the remaining business logic.
```

### Deep dive full refactor plan
```
!! Command: copilot-refactor
Phase: full
#PaymentOrchestrationService.java

Full refactor of this service.
Baseline first, then produce a phased plan with risk per step.
```

---

## 🧪 Command: copilot-test-generate

### Currently open file
```
Command: copilot-test-generate
#editor
```

### Specific method
```
Command: copilot-test-generate
#calculateDiscount
```

### Specific class
```
Command: copilot-test-generate
#DiscountService.java
```

### Concurrency tests for shared state
```
Command: copilot-test-generate
FocusAreas: concurrency
#RequestCounter.java
```

### Baseline test surface before generating
```
Command: copilot-test-generate
Phase: baseline
#CheckoutService.java

Map all testable paths, dependencies to mock,
and edge case inventory before generating any test code.
```

### Edge cases and exceptions only
```
Command: copilot-test-generate
FocusAreas: edge-cases, exceptions
#OrderService.java:processCheckout

Happy path tests already exist. Only edge and failure paths needed.
```

### Validation tests only — controller DTO
```
Command: copilot-test-generate
FocusAreas: validation
#CreateOrderRequest.java
#OrderController.java

Generate tests that verify all @Valid constraints fire correctly.
```

### Tests for selected code block
```
Command: copilot-test-generate
#selection
```

### Multiple files — integration boundary test
```
Command: copilot-test-generate
FocusAreas: happy-path, exceptions

Generate integration boundary tests between:
#OrderService.java
#InventoryClient.java
#PaymentClient.java
```

---

## 📖 Command: copilot-explain

### Currently open file
```
Command: copilot-explain
#editor
```

### Specific class
```
Command: copilot-explain
#OrderOrchestrator.java
```

### Specific method
```
Command: copilot-explain
#processCheckout
```

### Specific line range — unfamiliar logic
```
Command: copilot-explain
#OrderService.java:200-260

I inherited this section. Explain what it does before I touch it.
```

### Focus on data flow and concurrency
```
Command: copilot-explain
FocusAreas: data-flow, concurrency
#AsyncOrderDispatcher.java
```

### Plain English baseline first
```
Command: copilot-explain
Phase: baseline
#editor

Give me a plain English summary of what this does
before any technical breakdown.
```

### Explain a MyBatis XML mapper
```
Command: copilot-explain
FocusAreas: performance, security
#file:'src/main/resources/mapper/OrderMapper.xml'
```

### Explain across multiple files — full call chain
```
Command: copilot-explain
Phase: baseline

Explain the full request flow from HTTP entry to DB write:
#OrderController.java
#OrderService.java
#OrderMapper.java
```

### Deep dive on a complex orchestrator
```
!! Command: copilot-explain
Phase: full
#CheckoutOrchestrator.java

Full breakdown including all failure paths and concurrency risks.
```

---

## 🏛 Command: copilot-arch

### New system design from scratch
```
Command: copilot-arch

Design a notification service: email, SMS, push.
Must handle 10k events/sec with at-least-once delivery.
Stack: Spring Boot, Kafka, PostgreSQL.
```

### Baseline existing architecture before redesign
```
Command: copilot-arch
Phase: baseline
@workspace

Map the current architecture across the workspace.
Identify domains, coupling points, and shared data
before recommending any structural changes.
```

### Baseline from specific files representing current state
```
Command: copilot-arch
Phase: baseline

Map current architecture before proposing a split:
#OrderService.java
#InventoryService.java
#PaymentService.java
#UserService.java
```

### Focus on failure modes only
```
Command: copilot-arch
FocusAreas: failure-modes, scalability
#editor

Only analyze what breaks under load or partial failure.
We are not changing the design today.
```

### Deep dive full redesign
```
!! Command: copilot-arch
Phase: full
Scale: 20k req/sec
#InventoryReservationService.java

Full review and redesign proposal.
Include current state baseline, proposed design,
one alternative, and a phased migration plan.
```

---

## ⚡ Command: copilot-perf

### Currently open file
```
Command: copilot-perf
#editor

p99 latency: 4 seconds. SLA: 500ms.
```

### Specific service + mapper together
```
Command: copilot-perf
#OrderService.java
#OrderMapper.java

p99 latency: 4 seconds. SLA: 500ms.
```

### Baseline measurement mapping first
```
Command: copilot-perf
Phase: baseline
#CheckoutService.java

Map all hot paths, DB call counts, and blocking operations
before suggesting any fixes.
```

### Batch job — custom scale
```
Command: copilot-perf
Scale: batch
#NightlyReportJob.java

Processes 2M rows nightly. Takes 6 hours. Target: under 45 minutes.
```

### Focus on DB only
```
Command: copilot-perf
FocusAreas: database
#OrderService.java
#file:'src/main/resources/mapper/OrderMapper.xml'

Only analyze query patterns and index usage.
```

### Selected hot method only
```
Command: copilot-perf
FocusAreas: hot-path, algorithm
#selection
```

### Deep dive with profiling plan
```
!! Command: copilot-perf
Phase: full
#OrderProcessingService.java

Full performance audit.
Include baseline mapping, hot path analysis,
JVM concerns, and a concrete JFR profiling plan.
```

---

## 💡 Command: copilot-suggest

### Optimize currently open Dockerfile
```
Command: copilot-suggest
FocusAreas: size-reduction
#editor
```

### Dockerfile by path
```
Command: copilot-suggest
Phase: baseline
#file:'docker/Dockerfile'

Describe what the current Dockerfile costs before suggesting optimizations.
```

### Kubernetes manifest
```
Command: copilot-suggest
#file:'k8s/deployments/orders-deployment.yaml'

Add resource limits, liveness/readiness probes, and graceful shutdown config.
```

### Multiple manifests — full stack
```
Command: copilot-suggest
FocusAreas: optimization, safety

Review all deployment manifests:
#file:'k8s/deployments/orders-deployment.yaml'
#file:'k8s/deployments/inventory-deployment.yaml'
#file:'k8s/deployments/payments-deployment.yaml'
```

### Safe kubectl restart
```
Command: copilot-suggest

Safe kubectl command to restart all pods in the payments namespace.
```

### Safe git undo
```
Command: copilot-suggest
FocusAreas: safety, rollback

Safe git command to undo the last 3 commits already pushed to main.
```

### Maven build flags
```
Command: copilot-suggest
#file:'pom.xml'

Suggest build flags: skip tests but still run checkstyle and spotbugs.
```

---

## 🚚 Command: copilot-migrate

### RestTemplate → WebClient — scan whole workspace
```
Command: copilot-migrate
@workspace

Find all RestTemplate usages and produce a migration plan to WebClient.
```

### Baseline before migration
```
Command: copilot-migrate
Phase: baseline
@workspace

Map all current RestTemplate usages, coupling points,
and test coverage gaps before producing a migration plan.
```

### Java 8 → Java 17 — currently open file
```
Command: copilot-migrate
#editor

Modernize to Java 17+ idioms: records, sealed classes,
text blocks, pattern matching.
```

### XML Spring config → annotations
```
Command: copilot-migrate
Phase: full
#file:'src/main/resources/applicationContext.xml'

Full migration plan to annotation-based + Spring Boot auto-configuration.
Include baseline, steps, risks, and rollback plan per step.
```

### Focus on rollback plan only — mid-migration
```
Command: copilot-migrate
FocusAreas: rollback
#OrderMapper.java

We are mid-migration from MyBatis XML to JPA.
Only produce a rollback plan for remaining steps.
```

### Legacy module — anti-corruption layer
```
Command: copilot-migrate
Context: legacy-readonly
#LegacyBillingModule.java

Cannot modify this module.
Suggest an anti-corruption layer to isolate it from new code.
```

---

## 🔁 Command: copilot-session-start

### Full session with known context files
```
Command: copilot-session-start

Stack: Spring Boot 3.2, MyBatis, PostgreSQL 15
Scale: 8k req/sec peak
Concurrency: Virtual threads (Project Loom)
Deployment: Kubernetes on bare metal (Proxmox)
SLA: p99 < 300ms
Known debt: Legacy XML mappers without pagination, no distributed tracing yet

Key files for this session:
#OrderService.java
#InventoryService.java
#file:'src/main/resources/mapper/OrderMapper.xml'
```

### Minimal session — focused task
```
Command: copilot-session-start

Stack: Spring Boot 3.x, MySQL
Scale: low-traffic (internal tool)
Task: Refactor the reporting module only
Constraints: Cannot change DB schema

Focus file: #ReportingService.java
```

---

## 📜 Command: copilot-session-history

### End-of-session summary
```
Command: copilot-session-history
```

### Checklist format for team handoff
```
Command: copilot-session-history
OutputFormat: checklist
```

---

## 🔗 Combining Everything

### Maximum depth review — open file
```
!! Command: copilot-review
FocusAreas: database, concurrency
Phase: full
Scale: 10k req/sec
#editor
```

### Scoped performance review — table output
```
Command: copilot-perf
Scope: OrderService#processCheckout
FocusAreas: database, hot-path
OutputFormat: table
#OrderService.java
#OrderMapper.java
```

### Full architecture review — entire workspace
```
!! Command: copilot-arch
Phase: full
Scale: 20k req/sec
@workspace

Full architecture review and redesign proposal.
Include current state baseline, proposed design, and phased migration plan.
```

### Quick fix — selected code
```
? Command: copilot-suggest
#selection

What is the safest way to make this thread-safe?
```

### Greenfield test generation — specific file
```
Command: copilot-test-generate
FocusAreas: edge-cases, concurrency
Context: greenfield
#RateLimiterService.java
```

### Explain inherited code — baseline first
```
Command: copilot-explain
Phase: baseline
FocusAreas: data-flow, security
#editor

I inherited this class. Plain English summary first,
then technical breakdown.
```

### Cross-file data flow review — full chain
```
!! Command: copilot-review
FocusAreas: performance, database, data flow correctness
Phase: full

Analyze full request lifecycle from controller to DB:
#OrderController.java
#OrderService.java
#InventoryService.java
#OrderMapper.java
#file:'src/main/resources/mapper/OrderMapper.xml'
```
